---
title: Student & Instructor versions
---

Some teaching materials keep exercises and answers in one source document,
then render a learner-facing output and an answer-bearing output. This avoids
maintaining two copies of the same exercise text.

## Recommended setup for new projects

New course projects should use the [`quarto-teaching-tools`](https://github.com/au-mbg/quarto-teaching-tools)
extensions with two profiles:

- `student`: learner-facing output with `teaching.show-solutions: false`.
- `instructor`: answer-bearing output with `teaching.show-solutions: true`.

The `teaching.show-solutions` metadata setting is the switch read by the
versioning extensions. Profiles should set it explicitly rather than relying
on profile names alone.

```yaml
# _quarto-student.yml
teaching:
  show-solutions: false
```

```yaml
# _quarto-instructor.yml
teaching:
  show-solutions: true
  default-solution-title: "Solution"
```

## Solution callouts

Install and enable `callout-solution` when an explanation or worked answer
should appear only in instructor output. Write solution content as:

```markdown
::: {.callout-solution}
Answer text or worked solution.
:::
```

An individual solution can override the configured default title:

```markdown
::: {.callout-solution title="Worked example"}
Answer text or worked solution.
:::
```

## Conditional exercises and solutions

Install and enable `strip-solution` when a source document contains paired
student/instructor material. It handles both ordinary divs and code cells:

<pre><code>::: &#123;teaching="exercise"&#125;
Instructions or scaffolding retained in the student output.
:::

::: &#123;teaching="solution"&#125;
Answer content retained in the instructor output.
:::</code></pre>

<pre><code>```&#123;python&#125;
#| teaching: exercise
#| eval: false
... # Student starting point
```

```&#123;python&#125;
#| teaching: solution
print("Instructor solution")
```</code></pre>

Mark scaffold cells with `#| eval: false` explicitly when they should be
displayed but not executed. `strip-solution` does not add that setting.

## Check both views

When editing material affected by visibility rules:

1. Preview or render the learner-facing output and confirm answers are absent
   while instructions and starter material remain usable.
2. Preview or render the answer-bearing output and confirm worked solutions,
   code, and any instructor-specific guidance are visible.
3. Keep shared statements outside filtered blocks unless the text genuinely
   differs between audiences.

## Existing repositories

Maintain established repositories using their configured vocabulary and
filters. `structure_biology` already uses teaching-tools solution callouts,
collapsed callouts, and download buttons, with profiles named
`exercise`/`solution`. `molecular_biology_I` uses the recommended
`student`/`instructor` vocabulary but currently has project-specific filters.
`fysisk_biokemi` uses `student`/`master` and repository-local strip filters.

The legacy code-cell options `#| exercise: true` and `#| solution: true`
remain supported by `strip-solution` for existing material, but new content
should use `#| teaching: exercise` and `#| teaching: solution`.
